description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust wooden handle with a wrapped grip section, likely for improved handling and comfort. The handle is straight and cylindrical, showing a natural wood grain with a darker, possibly leather or cord-wrapped lower portion. The axe head is metallic, with a single broad, curved cutting edge and a pronounced beard, as well as a pointed poll opposite the blade. The blade has two circular holes, possibly for weight reduction or decorative purposes, and a slightly weathered, darkened finish. The head is mounted at a slight angle to the handle, suggesting a traditional wedged or pinned attachment. This tool appears well-suited for chopping or splitting tasks and could be found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance areas.
description: A hand axe with a straight wooden handle, wrapped grip, and a broad, bearded metal blade featuring two holes and a dark, weathered finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle, wrapped grip, and the broad, bearded blade with two holes.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the rectangular cross-section of the handle and the thin profile of the blade.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle, grip wrap, and the blade's beard and holes from the other side.
description_view_3: Handle-focused view from above, emphasizing the length and grip wrap, with the blade partially visible at the far end.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with a wrapped grip section, natural wood grain, and a slightly rectangular cross-section at the top.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad, curved edge with a pronounced beard, two circular holes, and a pointed poll; dark, weathered metal finish.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged or pinned mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle end.
